summary.intent: Re-land the GitHub App authentication PR (#2587) that was reverted due to rate limiting, fix the root cause (unauthenticated git operations in source_resolver.py), then address code review comments on the new PR #2709.
summary.outcome: Successfully created PR #2709 with GitHub App auth re-landed and source_resolver.py fixes. Identified and fixed 3 additional bugs from code review (rstrip bug, malformed auth URL, dead code branch). Dismissed 10 unnecessary CodeRabbit comments with detailed explanations.

repo: art-tools uses GitHub App authentication with per-org installation tokens, providing ~15,000 req/hr across 3 orgs vs 5,000/hr with a single PAT
ocp4-scan-konflux job clones 200+ repos and runs git ls-remote for each, easily hitting rate limits if unauthenticated (60/hr anonymous vs 5,000/hr authenticated)
source_resolver.py was missed in the original GitHub App auth migration (PR #2587), causing three methods to make unauthenticated GitHub requests
SSH agent auth (openshift-bot) coexists with HTTPS+GIT_ASKPASS auth for backward compatibility
Pre-commit hooks must not be bypassed with --no-verify; git add files before committing

code.finding: setup_and_fetch_public_upstream_source() passed only GIT_NO_PROMPTS to git fetch, missing GIT_ASKPASS auth env for HTTPS URLs
_get_remote_branch_ref() did git ls-remote with no set_env at all, relying only on SSH agent
_check_branch_protection() used raw Github(os.environ['GITHUB_TOKEN']) instead of get_github_client_for_org() for proper App/PAT auth
get_github_git_auth_env() already returns GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0 when credentials exist; no need to add it in callers
Cache key can be None by design - no org context means no org-specific caching, intentional behavior
rstrip('.git') strips individual chars {'.','g','i','t'}, breaking repo names like 'widget.git' → 'widge'; use removesuffix('.git') instead
Constructing f'https://github.com/{repo_name}' where repo_name lacks org creates malformed URL like 'https://github.com/kubernetes' instead of 'https://github.com/openshift-priv/kubernetes'
Dead else branch since --github-access-token is required=True, making the if condition always true

workflow: Git revert of a revert properly restores original changes (revert-of-revert pattern for re-landing PRs)
GitHub CLI gh pr create can use heredoc for PR body; write to temp file if complex markdown with quotes
GitHub API for PR comment replies: POST to /repos/{owner}/{repo}/pulls/comments/{comment_id}/replies
Always git add files before committing; avoid --no-verify unless explicitly requested
Reply to code review comments to provide context on dismissed findings, not just ignore them

friction: Accidentally switched to main branch instead of working branch (re-land-github-app-auth), causing confusion when reading file contents
CodeRabbit posted 12+ comments requiring manual triage to separate real bugs from false positives
GitHub CLI heredoc syntax required writing to temp file due to quote escaping issues in markdown tables
Pre-existing test failures (5 macOS symlink failures in test_util.py) unrelated to changes
open_items: PR #2709 is on hold (/hold) until a live ocp4-scan-konflux test run confirms no rate limiting occurs
Monitor Jenkins logs to verify GIT_ASKPASS appears in env vars for all git fetch/ls-remote operations

CodeRabbit follow-up suggestions deferred: cache remote migration on SSH→HTTPS transition, PAT fallback wrapper on App auth failures, PR URL parsing refactor, early auth validation in prepare_release_konflux
The 3 new fixes (config.py rstrip, scan_sources_konflux.py auth URL, images_okd.py dead branch) are staged but not yet committed/pushed
